purpose: Core context store — CRUD over per-project JSON files in ~/.ai-context/. Owns context lifecycle (create, normalize, save, archive, restore, delete), action compaction with per-field memory caps (b/d/c/f/h), legacy schema migration, project scanning, search, and template management.
invariants: createdAt is set once on creation and preserved across saves; lastUsed is always overwritten with the current ISO timestamp.
perms.allow migrates from legacy perms.claude on read — old projects do not need a manual rename.

perms.codexSandboxMode is a tri-state enum ('workspace-write' | 'danger-full-access' | null); invalid values coerce to null. Legacy perms.sandboxMode boolean is dropped on normalize without migration (cleanup happened in v4.1.0).
normalizeContext bounds memory list lengths (b ≤ 15, d/c ≤ 20, f ≤ 30, h ≤ 12); overflow on actions triggers compactActions which folds older actions into h.
Context version `v` is always upgraded to ≥ DEFAULT_CONTEXT_VERSION on read; corrupted files fall back to defaults rather than throwing.
gotchas: normalizePath strips trailing slashes — root equality comparisons (e.g. scanAndCreateContexts) must round-trip through it before comparing.
archiveContext appends `_<timestamp>` to the name on collision with no user override of the suffix format.
loadContext on a malformed JSON file returns a default context with `e: "ctx_parse_err"` — masking the real error; check `e` to detect.
searchContexts is a linear scan with weighted scoring (name > root > note > files/decisions/constraints); no index — fine at < 1000 contexts.
